mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate directly reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing present value of equity claims. A sustained decrease in FEDFUNDS over 3 months signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns within the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2008 crisis period with elevated policy rates
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following financial stress
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns in easing periods do not exceed baseline by at least 50 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which increases aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS above 100k jobs triggers positive equity returns within 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:November 2009 labor market recovery phase post-crisis
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) expansion reflects consumer spending strength and forward-looking demand conditions. When RSAFS growth accelerates above its 6-month trend, equity valuations expand due to improved earnings visibility. This mechanism operates over a 60-day horizon as market reprices growth trajectories.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth rate exceeds 6-month average by > 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Post-crisis consumer spending recovery in late 2009
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth periods underperform low-growth periods by > 200 basis points (t-test p < 0.05 in wrong direction).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction signals weakening supply-side capacity utilization and forward earnings pressure. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, equity markets reprice downward within 20 trading days as investors anticipate margin compression and reduced output growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 (fails to show negative correlation).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and spending capacity. This joint deterioration triggers negative equity returns within 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp AND PAYEMS declines > 50,000 in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market stabilization in late 2009 post-crisis
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary pressure
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) expansion combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals broad-based demand recovery across residential and consumer sectors. This dual expansion triggers positive equity returns over 60 trading days as market reprices cyclical growth and construction-related earnings.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average > prior 6-month average AND RSAFS 3-month average > prior 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:Housing and retail recovery in 2009 post-crisis period
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Dual-signal instances produce positive 60-day returns in < 58% of cases, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) when controlling for individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) acceleration above 2.5% annual rate combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4%) signals monetary tightening regime where real discount rates rise, compressing equity valuations. This regime produces negative 20-day returns as market reprices cost of capital upward.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-crisis inflation and policy rate environment in late 2009
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Tightening regime produces negative 20-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term fails to improve model fit (R² improvement < 0.02) over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expansion signals increasing capacity utilization and forward earnings growth. In post-crisis recovery regimes, INDPRO acceleration above 1% month-over-month produces positive 5-day equity returns as market reprices supply-side strength and production capacity improvements.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 12-month rolling window.
